theme: The PM Role — What You Actually Do
brief: Understand what a PM actually does day-to-day — strategy, execution, and stakeholder alignment. Analyze a real JD and compare it to ground reality at Indian startups.
Learn how to deeply understand users through interviews and surveys. Map pain points using Jobs-to-be-Done and build an empathy map for a Zepto user.
Define the problem precisely before jumping to solutions. Write a crisp problem statement, and practice How Might We framing using CRED's late-payment challenge.
Build conviction around your solution with a structured hypothesis. Learn assumption mapping and prioritize what to validate first.
Turn user insights into a product roadmap. Learn Now/Next/Later framing and MoSCoW prioritization using a Meesho seller tool scenario.
Write PRDs that engineers actually want to read. Cover user stories, acceptance criteria, and edge cases with a PhonePe feature as the case study.
Understand the trade-offs between native, web, and hybrid apps. Learn mobile-first design principles and review a Razorpay checkout flow.
Translate product requirements into wireframes and flows. Practice low-fi wireframing for a new Zomato order-tracking screen.
Learn the metrics that matter — activation, retention, DAU/MAU, and NPS. Define success for a CRED reward feature and build a north-star metric framework.
Set up and run A/B experiments the right way. Understand statistical significance, sample size, and how Swiggy tests new features.
Align engineering, design, and business around a single launch plan. Build a lightweight GTM doc and launch checklist.
Prioritize ruthlessly using RICE and ICE scoring. Compare feature requests from sales, support, and users — and make the call.
Understand how Indian payment rails work — UPI, wallets, BNPL. Map the checkout journey and identify drop-off points for a D2C brand.
Learn B2B product nuances — longer sales cycles, multi-stakeholder buying, and enterprise contracts. Analyze a Razorpay B2B dashboard case.
Deep-dive into growth loops and viral coefficients. Design a referral mechanic for a fintech app using Zepto's grocery + credit play.
Build your data intuition — SQL basics for PMs, funnel analysis, and cohort retention. Read a retention chart and find the drop-off.
Understand the PM × Design relationship. Learn how to give useful design feedback without micromanaging, using Figma handoff as context.
Navigate engineering trade-offs — tech debt, scalability, and build vs buy. Practice a build/buy/partner decision for a logistics feature.
Prepare for a PM interview — product sense, estimation, and execution questions. Walk through a full mock with an Indian startup prompt.
Build your product strategy muscle — vision, bets, and OKRs. Write a 6-month strategy for a hypothetical Indian edtech product.
Capstone day — synthesize everything into a mini product pitch. Present a problem, solution, roadmap, and metrics for a product of your choice.
